---
title: "LRP83: Period-resolved dose-response — intervention dose -> letter sounds (LS)"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_gate_badge.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_key_findings.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_reading_guide.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP83 is the letter-sound companion to [`lrp-rli-dose-077`](../lrp-rli-dose-077/) (word reading), giving the dose-response family coverage of the two _largest_ ITT effects — letter sounds (`L`) and phoneme blending (`B`) — which the word-reading-only family lacked. The estimand, causal structure and caveats are identical to LRP77; only the outcome changes.

**Estimand.** How letter-sound _conditional change_ relates to the per-period intervention **dose** (`attend`, sessions attended that period), with partial pooling across the three periods, and whether that dose–gain slope **varies by period**. The outcome is the Beta-Binomial post-count of $L$ conditional on its own baseline logit ($N = 32$) — conditional change, **not** a raw change score (Lord's paradox / regression to the mean). All three phase transitions are stacked with phase-specific intercepts; the dose enters standardised:

$$
\eta = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{phase}[p]}
     + \beta_G\,G
     + \gamma_{\text{own}}\,\text{logit}(L_{\text{pre}})
     + \gamma_A\,z(\text{age})
     + u_{\text{child}}
     + \beta_{\text{dose}[p]}\,z(\text{attend})
$$

with $\beta_{\text{dose}[p]} = \mu_{\text{dose}} + \sigma_{\text{dose}}\,z_p$ (partial-pooled period slopes).

## Adjustment set (revised DAG)

The focal edge is $\text{dose} \to \text{outcome}$; the back-door set is $\{G, L_{\text{pre}}, A\}$ — $G$ (arm, coded $G = 2-\text{group}$, positive = benefit) is the sole confounder blocking $\text{dose} \leftarrow \text{intervention} \to \text{outcome}$; $L_{\text{pre}}$ is the autoregression / RTM control (parameterisation, not back-door); age is a maturation precision covariate (no $\text{age} \to \text{dose}$ edge). **Cumulative dose** (`attend_cumul`) is a descendant of the `IS` collider and is **not** conditioned on (reopens the latent-ability back door, #269). The waitlist controls' period-1 `attend == 0` rows are kept as the zero-dose anchor.

_Adjusted association, not \._ Dose is **not** randomised (only `intervention` is), so this is an adjusted association. The load-bearing assumption is no $\text{ability} \to \text{dose}$ edge, but Frank's 2012 caveat (the least-able children attended least) is exactly that edge; the subject random intercept absorbs only _stable_ child differences. Read the dose slope with that in mind — the randomised causal effect for letter sounds lives in the ITT suite (`lrp-rli-itt-007`).

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_results_dose_response.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_technical.qmd >}}
{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
